12. Meat Room temperature °C -11 to -13 {{usersFour.no1h_75}} {{usersFour.no2h_75}}

(K) Sea Water flow rate m3/hr 4-4.5 {{usersEight.per_30TPD_sea_water_disc_1}} {{usersEight.per_30TPD_sea_water_disc_2}} {{usersEight.per_30TPD_sea_water_disc_3}} {{usersEight.per_30TPD_sea_water_disc_4}}

FUEL SYSTEM

1. Fuel Transfer Pump

(a). Carry out following checks before starting the pump.

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Check pipe system is clean and clear from obstruction {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pipe_sys}}
(ii) Prime the pumps with fuel oil drawn from the supply system to ensure that the pump will not run in a dry unlubricated condition. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_prime_pump}}
(iii) Rotate the pumps, by hand, in direction indicated on the driving end cover. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_end_cover}}
(iv) Check that the functional position of all valves should correspond with the valve position indicators. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valve_pos}}
(v) Check that all valves are in the open position. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_open_pos}}
(vii) Ensure all deck fuel receiving valve should be closed {{usersEight.fuel_trans_deck_closed}}
(viii) Switch ON starter and check rotation of motor {{usersEight.fuel_trans_switch_on}}
(ix) Check pump for sign of overheating over a 10minute period {{usersEight.fuel_trans_10min_prd}}
(x) Open suction from one of the Aft tanks No. 10 and 22 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_Aft_tanks}}
(xi) Repeat the above fuel transfer procedure for aft fuel tanks no. 10 to 22 and check time taken from start to pump working {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pump_working}}
(xii) During fuel transfer check operation of Sounding tubes, TL 15 and record figures {{usersEight.fuel_trans_TL_15}}

(2). Check – Off List for System Trials

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(a) Pump/ centrifuge installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings Pump No.1 {{usersEight.check_off_centrifuge_pump_1}}
Pump No.2 {{usersEight.check_off_centrifuge_pump_2}}
Fuel Separator no.1 {{usersEight.check_off_centrifuge_fuel_1}}
Fuel Separator no.2 {{usersEight.check_off_centrifuge_fuel_2}}
(b) Pressure gauges and thermometers are calibrated and red marked indicating working pressure of the system. Pump 1 suction pressure gauges {{usersEight.check_off_therm_pump_suc_1}}
Pump 1 discharge pressure gauges {{usersEight.check_off_therm_pump_disc_1}}
Pump 2 suction pressure gauges {{usersEight.check_off_therm_pump_suc_2}}
Pump 2 discharge pressure gauges {{usersEight.check_off_therm_pump_disc_2}}
Separator feed pump suction gauges {{usersEight.check_off_therm_sep_suc_1}}
Separator feed pump discharge gauges {{usersEight.check_off_therm_sep_disc_2}}
Separator 2 feed pump suction gauges {{usersEight.check_off_therm_sep_suc_2}}
(c) All FO tanks and FO Service tanks cleaned,inspected and cleared by respective agencies and ready to receive fuel oil.(NA for PRT) Storage tank no.1 {{usersEight.check_off_fO_tank_stor_1}}
Storage tank no.2 {{usersEight.check_off_fO_tank_stor_2}}
Storage tank no.3 {{usersEight.check_off_fO_tank_stor_3}}
Storage tank no.4 {{usersEight.check_off_fO_tank_stor_4}}
Storage tank no. 5 Storage tank no. 6 , 9, 10, 11, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20 , 21 and 22 {{usersEight.check_off_fO_tank_stor_22}}
Fuel oil service tank no.1 & 2 {{usersEight.check_off_fO_tank_fuel_oil_1_2}}
Fuel oil service tank no.3 & 4 {{usersEight.check_off_ffO_tank_fuel_oil_3_4}}
(d) Sufficient lights available. {{usersEight.check_off_suff_light}}
(e) Communication control position available between and tanks. (Motorola) pump {{usersEight.check_off_comm_moto}}
(f) Test area is cleaned. {{usersEight.check_off_test_area}}
(g) All the pipes are clamps properly supported with {{usersEight.check_off_pipe_clamps}}
(h) Talley plates are gauges fitted on all valves, and gauge. {{usersEight.check_off_talley_plate}}
(j) Portable Fire Fighting extinguisher as per CNAL. {{usersEight.check_off_fire_CNAL}}
(k) Air escape pipes of all FO tanks are open/clear of any obstruction (prior to filling of Tanks). Storage tank no.1 {{usersEight.check_off_stor_tank_1}}
Storage tank no.2 {{usersEight.check_off_stor_tank_2}}
Storage tank no.3 {{usersEight.check_off_stor_tank_3}}
Storage tank no.4 {{usersEight.check_off_stor_tank_4}}
Storage tank no. 5 Storage tank no. 6 , 9, 10, 11, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20 , 21 and 22 {{usersEight.check_off_stor_tank_22}}
Fuel oil service tank no.1 & 2 {{usersEight.check_off_fuel_oil_1_2}}
Fuel oil service tank no.3 & 4 {{usersEight.check_off_fuel_oil_3_4}}

System Trials.

(a). Piping

Visual checks of following components to be carried out for any leakages or damage.

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Compensators {{usersEight.fuel_trans_ppip_compen}}
(ii) Flange-connections {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_flange}}
(iii) Gaskets {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_gasket}}
(iv) Adapters {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_adap}}
(v) Suction strainers - for cleanliness and correctfittings. Pump No.1 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_strain_pump_1}}
Pump No.2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_strain_pump_2}}
Separator feed pump no.1 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_feed_pump_1}}
Separator feed pump no.2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_feed_pump_2}}
(vi) System Integrity checks {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_integrity_check}}
(vii) Supporting clamps {{usersEight.fuel_trans_pip_supp_clamps}}

(b). Valves

Visual and operational checks of following valves to be carried out. Operate the valves through their complete range of operations to ensure that the valve position indicator moves with the valve..

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Pump valves suction Pump No.1 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_suc_pump_1}}
Pump No.2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_suc_pump_2}}
Fuel Separator no.1 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_sep_1}}
Fuel Separator no.2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_sep_2}}
(ii) Pump discharge valves Pump No. 1 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_disc_pump_1}}
Pump No.2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_disc_pump_2}}
Separator feed pump no.1 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_sep_feed_pump_1}}
Separator feed pump no.2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_sep_feed_pump_2}}
(iii) Tank fuelling and defueling valves Storage tank no.1 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_def_tank_1}}
Storage tank no.2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_def_tank_2}}
Storage tank no.3 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_def_tank_3}}
Storage tank no.4 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_def_tank_4}}
Storage tank no. 5 Storage tank no. 6 , 9, 10, 11, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20 , 21 and 22 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_tank5_22}}
Fuel oil service tank no.1 & 2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_oil_tank_1_2}}
Fuel oil service tank no.3 & 4 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_oil_tank_3_4}}
(v) Main fuelling valves (fitted on upper deck) FWD fuelling (AK100 fwd and rear) {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_FWD_AK100}}
Port drum space {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_port_drum}}
Starboard drum space {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_drum_space}}
Aft fuelling (helo deck) {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_AFT_deck}}
(vi) Balancing valves Balancing valves between fuel oil tank no. 3&4, 8&9, 10&11, 14&15, 16 & 17, 18 & 19. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_bal_19}}
(vii) Service tank filling and suction valves Fuel oil service tank no. 1 & 2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_tank_1_2}}
Fuel oil service tank no. 3 & 4 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_tank_3_4}}
(viii) Separator feed pump suction and discharge valves Feed pump no.1 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_feed_pump_1}}
Feed pump no.2 {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_feed_pump_2}}
(ix) Separator system inlet, outlet, isolating and service tank filling valves {{usersEight.fuel_trans_valves_sep_inlet}}

(c) Tanks. Visual/ functional checks to be carried out for the following.

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Sounding tube and sounding tapes – Volumetric marking on sounding chart to be cross checked with sounding tape markings during fuelling. Intactness of sounding tape and tube to be checked during tank cleaning activity. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_soun_tube}}
(ii) Manhole door gaskets to be renewed. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_gasket_renewed}}

(d)Instrumentation.
Visual/ functional checks to be carried out for the following and test certificate to be obtained.

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Pressure gauges. All pressure and vacuum gauges in the system and equipment to checked for functionality and operation. Pressure gauge marking for operating range to be completed.Calibration certificates to be provided. Valves in gauge line and on the pressure gauge is to be checked for functionality and operation. Pump no.1 suction pressure gauge {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_pump_suc_1}}
Pump no.1 discharge pressure gauge {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_pump_disc_1}}
Pump no.2 suction pressure gauge {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_pump_suc_2}}
Pump no.2 discharge pressure gauge {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_pump_disc_2}}
Separator feed pump no. 1 suction gauge {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_feed_suc_1}}
Separator feed pump no. 1 discharge gauge {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_feed_disc_1}}
Separator feed pump no. 2 suction gauge {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_feed_suc_2}}
Separator feed pump no. 2 discharge gauge {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_feed_disc_2}}
other gauges {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_press_other_gauges}}
(ii) Sensor. All sensors in the tanks to be checked for functionality and operation. Calibration certificates to be provided Storage tank no.1 level sensor {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_sensor_stor_tank_1}}
Storage tank no.2 level sensor {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_sensor_stor_tank_2}}
Storage tank no.3 level sensor {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_sensor_stor_tank_3}}
Storage tank no.4 level sensor {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_sensor_stor_tank_4}}
Storage tank no. 5 ,6,9,10,11,14,15,16,17, 18, 19,20,21 & 22 level sensor {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_sensor_stor_tank_22}}
Service tank no.1& 2 level sensor {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_sensor_stor_tank_1_2}}
Service tank no. 3 & 4 level sensor {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_sensor_stor_tank_3_4}}
(iii) Gauge lines. All gauge lines to be checked for proper fitting and leakages {{usersEight.fuel_trans_inst_sensor_gauge_line}}

(e) Foundation.
Visual checks of foundation to be carried out ascertaining its condition.

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Foundation Fuel Transfer Pumps {{usersEight.fuel_trans_found_pump}}
Fuel feed pumps {{usersEight.fuel_trans_found_feed_pump}}
Separator {{usersEight.fuel_trans_found_sep}}

(f) Mounting.
Condition, attenuation and grouping of mounts to be checked.

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Mounting Physical Condition {{usersEight.fuel_trans_mount_phy_cond}}
Attenuation {{usersEight.fuel_trans_mount_atten}}
Service life as per MAINTOPs/OEM Documents. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_mount_OEM_doc}}

(g) Functionality Checks.
Functionality checks of system (fueling and defueling lines) is to be carried out as follows..

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Fuelling. Functionality checks/ Intactness of the fueling line to be checked during fueling from barge/ ship at harbour. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_func_barge_ship}}
(ii) Goose necks of foxle fueling points (Port) and pressure gauges to be checked for proper fitment and working. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_func_goose_neck}}
(iii) Port and Stbd torpedo deck fuelling points (angle valve) to be checked for proper fitment and intactness. {{usersEight.fuel_trans_func_port_stbd}}

AIR SYSTEM

1. Pre-Requisite Check – Off List for System Trials.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(a) Compressor installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings HPAC no. 1 {{usersEight.air_system_compre_HPAC1}}
HPAC no. 2 {{usersEight.air_system_compre_HPAC2}}
HPAC no. 3 {{usersEight.air_system_compre_HPAC3}}
DD Air Compresor {{usersEight.air_system_compre_HPAC4}}
(b) System/ installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings. {{usersEight.system_compre}}
(c) Sufficient lights available. {{usersEight.Sufficient_light}}
(d) Communication available between compressor control position and bottles. (Motorola) {{usersEight.comm_compre_HPAC1}}
(e) Test area is cleaned {{usersEight.test_area_clean}}
(f) All the pipes are properly supported with clamps. {{usersEight.pipes_clamps}}
(g) Talley plates are fitted on all valves, cocks and gauges {{usersEight.talley_plats_gauge}}

2. Electric Compressor Plant (HPAC 1, 2 & 3).

a. Carry out following checks before starting the compressor.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(i) Check pipe system is clean and clear from obstruction. {{usersEight.Electri_compress_pipe_sys}}
(ii) Check air intake filter for cleanliness, correct fitting and free from any type of obstruction. {{usersEight.Electri_compress_air_intake}}
(iii) Rotate the compressor, by hand, in direction indicated on the driving end cover. {{usersEight.Electri_compress_rotate_compre}}
(iv) Check air bottle drain valves to be closed. {{usersEight.Electri_compress_air_bottle}}
(v) Check air bottle inlet and outlet valves to be opened {{usersEight.Electri_compress_air_bottle_inlet}}
(vi) All valves are in the system are in open position. {{usersEight.Electri_compress_air_valves}}
(vii) Check tightness of compressor foundation bolts {{usersEight.Electri_compress_tightness}}
(viii) Check sump oil level of compressor with the help of dip stick and lubricator oil level. {{usersEight.Electri_compress_sump_oil_level}}
(ix) Check surrounding of compressor to be free from any type of obstruction. {{usersEight.Electri_compress_surrounding}}
(x) Check fresh water level in tank and Start the compressor and check correct rotation. {{usersEight.Electri_compress_fresh_water_level}}

b.Following checks carried out Compressor is running condition.

(i) Performance Checks       Performance Trials - {{usersEight.perfor_trial}}

(ii) Capacity Trials

Ser. Description Unit HPAC No. 1 HPAC No. 2 HPAC No. 3
Initial Reading Final Reading Initial Reading Final Reading Initial Reading Final Reading
(aa) Air Bottle Pressure Bar {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Air_bottle1}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Air_bottle2}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Air_bottle3}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Air_bottle4}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Air_bottle5}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Air_bottle6}}
(ab) Time Taken Min {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Time_taken1}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Time_taken2}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Time_taken3}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Time_taken4}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Time_taken5}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_Time_taken6}}
(ac) Capacity achieved. M3/hr {{usersEight.Perform_checks_capacity1}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_capacity2}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_capacity3}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_capacity4}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_capacity5}} {{usersEight.Perform_checks_capacity6}}

3. Diesel Driven Compressor

a. Carry out following checks before starting the compressor.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(i) Check pipe system is clean and clear from obstruction. {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_1}}
(ii) Check air intake filter for cleanliness, correct fitting and free from any type of obstruction. {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_2}}
(iii) Rotate the compressor, by hand, in direction indicated on the driving end cover. {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_3}}
(iv) Check air bottle drain valves to be closed. {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_4}}
(v) Check air bottle inlet and outlet valves to be opened {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_5}}
(vi) All valves are in the system are in open position. {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_6}}
(vii) Check tightness of compressor foundation bolts {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_7}}
(viii) Check sump oil level of compressor with the help of dip stick and lubricator oil level. {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_8}}
(ix) Check surrounding of compressor to be free from any type of obstruction. {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_9}}
(x) Check fresh water level in tank and Start the compressor and check correct rotation. {{usersEight.Diesel_driven_10}}

(b) Piping. Visual checks of following components to be carried out for any leakages or damage.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(i) Pipelines {{usersEight.Piping_pipelines}}
(ii) Flange-connections {{usersEight.Piping_Flange}}
(iii) Gaskets {{usersEight.Piping_Gaskets}}
(iv) Adapters {{usersEight.Piping_Adapters}}
(v) Suction filter - for cleanliness and correct fittings HPAC No. 1 {{usersEight.Piping_Suction_HPAC1}}
HPAC No. 2 {{usersEight.Piping_Suction_HPAC2}}
HPAC No. 3 {{usersEight.Piping_Suction_HPAC3}}
Diesel Driven Air Compressor {{usersEight.Piping_Suction_Diesel_driven}}
(vi) System Integrity checks - for any type of leakages. Complete starting air system is to be pressurised up to 200 bar pressure {{usersEight.Piping_System_Integrity}}
(vii) Supporting clamps - Sufficient supporting clamps to be provided at regular intervals as per NES 865. Clamps should have rubber inserts and adequate fasteners. End connection of supporting frame should have rigid support {{usersEight.Piping_Supporting_clamps}}
(viii) System pipe line to be checked upto working pressure for 2 hrs. {{usersEight.Piping_System_pipe}}

(c) Valves.
Visual and operational checks of following valves are to be carried out. Operate the valves through their complete range of operations to ensure that the valve position indicator moves with the valve.

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Compressor discharge valves HPAC No. 1 {{usersEight.Valves_compressor_hPAC_no01}}
HPAC No. 2 {{usersEight.Valves_compressor_hPAC_no02}}
HPAC No. 3 {{usersEight.Valves_compressor_hPAC_no03}}
Diesel Driven Air Compressor {{usersEight.Valves_compressor_hPAC_no04}}
(ii) Air Bottle filing valves HPAC air bottles no 1,2,3,4 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bottle_hPAC_no01}}
HPAC air bottles no 5,6,7,8 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bottle_hPAC_no02}}
HPAC air bottles no 9,10,11,12 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bottle_hPAC_no03}}
HPAC air bottles no 13,14,15,16 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bottle_hPAC_no04}}
(iii) Air Bottle outlet valves HPAC air bottles no 1,2,3,4 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bot_outlet_hPAC_no01}}
HPAC air bottles no 5,6,7,8 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bot_outlet_hPAC_no02}}
HPAC air bottles no 9,10,11,12 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bot_outlet_hPAC_no03}}
HPAC air bottles no 13,14,15,16 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bot_outlet_hPAC_no04}}
(iv) Air Bottle drain valves HPAC air bottles no 1,2,3,4 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bot_drain_hPAC_no01}}
HPAC air bottles no 5,6,7,8 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bot_drain_hPAC_no02}}
HPAC air bottles no 9,10,11,12 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bot_drain_hPAC_no03}}
HPAC air bottles no 13,14,15,16 {{usersEight.Valves_air_bot_drain_hPAC_no04}}
(v) Supply in inlet and outlet valve of reducers {{usersEight.Valves_supply_hPAC_no01}}
(vi) Isolating valves {{usersEight.Valves_isolating_hPAC_no01}}
(vi) Safety/ relief valves fitted on compressor and reservoir. Test certificate to be provided. HPAC No. 1 {{usersEight.Valves_safety_compr_hPAC_no01}}
HPAC No. 2 {{usersEight.Valves_safety_compr_hPAC_no02}}
HPAC No. 3 {{usersEight.Valves_safety_compr_hPAC_no03}}
Diesel Driven Air Compressor {{usersEight.Valves_safety_compr_hPAC_no04}}

(d) Instrumentation.
Visual/ functional checks are to be carried out for the following:

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Pressure gauges.
All pressure gauges in the system and equipment to checked for functionality and operation.
Pressure gauge marking for operating range to be completed.
Calibration certificates to be provided Valves in gauge line and on the pressure, gauge are to be checked for functionality and operation .		 HPAC no. 1 discharge Pr gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_01}}
HPAC no. 2 discharge Pr gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_02}}
HPAC no. 3 discharge Pr gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_03}}
DD air compressor discharge pr gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_04}}
HP air bottle no.1,2,3,4 Pr gauges {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_05}}
HP air bottle no.5,6,7,8 Pr gauges {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_06}}
HP air bottle no.9,10,11,12 Pr gauges {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_07}}
HP air bottle no.13,14,15, 16 Pr gauges {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_08}}
All Reducers inlet and outlet Pr gauges {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_09}}
(ii) Pressure switches. All pressure switches in the system and equipment to be checked for functionality and operation. Auto cut-in and cut-off of compressor to be checked. Calibration certificates to be provided. HPAC no. 1 discharge Pr gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_10}}
HPAC no. 2 discharge Pr gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_11}}
HPAC no. 3 discharge Pr gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_12}}
Diesel Driven Air compressor {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_13}}
(iii) Sensors. All sensors in the system and related equipment to be checked for functionality and operation. Calibration certificates to be provided Both cruise GTs bottles pressure sensor {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_14}}
Both boost GTs bottles pressure sensor {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_15}}
HPAC no. 1 running indication in MCR {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_16}}
HPAC no. 2 running indication in MCR {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_17}}
HPAC no. 3 running indication in MCR {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_18}}
(iv) Gauge lines. All gauge lines to be checked for proper fitting and leakages. DD air compressor discharge pr gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_pressure_remark_19}}

(e) Foundation.
Visual checks of foundation are to be done for ascertaining foundation condition.

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Foundation HPAC no. 1 {{usersEight.e_foundation_remark_01}}
HPAC no. 2 {{usersEight.e_foundation_remark_02}}
HPAC no. 3 {{usersEight.e_foundation_remark_03}}
Diesel Driven Air Compressor {{usersEight.e_foundation_remark_04}}
HP air bottle no.1,2,3,4 {{usersEight.e_foundation_remark_05}}
HP air bottle no.5,6,7,8 {{usersEight.e_foundation_remark_06}}
HP air bottle no.9,10,11,12 {{usersEight.e_foundation_remark_07}}
HP air bottle no.13,14,15,16 {{usersEight.e_foundation_remark_08}}

(f) Mounting.
Condition, attenuation and grouping of mounts have to be checked.

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Mounting Physical Condition {{usersEight.f_mounting_remark_01}}
Attenuation {{usersEight.f_mounting_remark_02}}
Service life as per MAINTOPS/OEM Doc. {{usersEight.f_mounting_remark_03}}

LUB OIL SYSTEM

1. Pre-Requisite Check – Off List for System Trials.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(a) Pump/ centrifuge installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings. Applicable in case of ABER/ MLU replacement of item GT Lub oil Transfer pump {{usersEight.lub_oil_sys_GTa}}
RG Lub oil Transfer pump {{usersEight.lub_oil_sys_RGa}}
GT LO Purifier {{usersEight.lub_oil_sys_GT_LOa}}
RG LO Purifier {{usersEight.lub_oil_sys_RG_LOa}}
(b) Pressure gauges and thermometers are calibrated and red marked indicating working pressure of the system. Pump suction pressure gauge {{usersEight.lub_oil_press_pump1b}}
Pump discharge pressure gauge {{usersEight.lub_oil_press_pump2b}}
Purifier feed pump suction gauges {{usersEight.lub_oil_press_pump3b}}
Purifier feed pump discharge gauge {{usersEight.lub_oil_press_pump4b}}
Purifier outlet pressure gauge {{usersEight.lub_oil_press_pump5b}}
(c) System/ installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings. {{usersEight.lub_oil_sys_install}}
(d) The purifier sludge outlet pipe must not be at an angle of more than 300 from the vertical plane. {{usersEight.lub_oil_purifier_outlet}}
(e) The operating water tank should be placed as closed to the separator as possible and in accordance with the drawing. {{usersEight.lub_oil_operating}}
(f) The discharge from relief valve is to be directed to safe position. {{usersEight.lub_oil_discharge_valve}}
(g) LO purifier heating pipes are to be insulated. {{usersEight.lub_oil_purife_pipe}}
(h) The lub oil storage tank is cleaned, inspected and cleared by respective agencies and ready to receive lub oil. {{usersEight.lub_oil_storage_tank}}
(j) Sufficient lights available. {{usersEight.lub_oil_suff_lights}}
(k) Communication available between pump control position and tanks. (Motorola) {{usersEight.lub_oil_comm_pumps}}
(l) Test area is cleaned. {{usersEight.lub_oil_test_Area}}
(m) All the pipes are properly supported with clamps {{usersEight.lub_oil_pipe_clamp}}
(n) Talley plates are fitted on all valves, cocks and gauges. {{usersEight.lub_oil_tally_plate}}
(p) Air escape pipes are open/clear of any obstruction (prior to filling of Tanks) {{usersEight.lub_oil_air_escape_obs}}

2. Lub Oil Transfer Pump.

a. Carry out following checks before starting the pump.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(i) Check pipe system is clean and clear from obstruction. {{usersEight.lub_oil_compress_pipe_sys}}
(ii) Prime the pump with oil drawn from the supply system to ensure that the pump will not run in a dry unlubricated condition {{usersEight.lub_oil_compress_air_intake}}
(iii) Rotate the pump, by hand, in direction indicated on the driving end cover. {{usersEight.lub_oil_compress_rotate_compre}}
(iv) Check that all valves are in the open position. {{usersEight.lub_oil_compress_valve_bottle}}
(v) Start the pump and check correct rotation. {{usersEight.lub_oil_start_pump}}

3. System Trials.

(a) Piping. Visual checks of following components to be carried out for any leakages or damage.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(i) Compensators {{usersEight.Piping_lub_oil_Compen}}
(ii) Flange-connections {{usersEight.Piping_lub_oil_Flange}}
(iii) Gaskets {{usersEight.Piping_lub_oil_Gaskets}}
(iv) Adapters {{usersEight.Piping_lub_oil_Adapters}}
(v) Suction strainers - for cleanliness and correct fittings {{usersEight.Piping_lub_oil_Suction_HPAC1}}
(vi) System Integrity checks - for any type of leakages. (To be undertaken at working pressure during normal operation of system) {{usersEight.Piping_lub_oil_System_Integrity}}
(vii) Supporting clamps - Sufficient supporting clamps to be provided at regular intervals as per NES 865. Clamps should have rubber inserts and adequate fasteners. End connection of supporting frame should have rigid support {{usersEight.Piping_lub_oil_Supporting_clamps}}

(b) Valves.
Visual and operational checks of following valves are to be carried out. Operate the valves through their complete range of operations to ensure that the valve position indicator moves with the valve.

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Pump 1 suction Valve {{usersEight.LO_Pump_syction_valve01}}
(ii) Pump 1 discharge Valve {{usersEight.LO_Pump_syction_valve02}}
(iii) Pump 2 suction Valve {{usersEight.LO_Pump_syction_valve03}}
(iv) Pump 2 discharge Valve {{usersEight.LO_Pump_syction_valve04}}
(v) Tank filling valve. {{usersEight.LO_Pump_syction_valve05}}
(vi) Tank de-filling valve. {{usersEight.LO_Pump_syction_valve06}}
(vii) Isolating/filing valves GT-1 Storage Tanks {{usersEight.LO_Isolating_valve01}}
GT-2 Storage Tanks {{usersEight.LO_Isolating_valve02}}
GT-3 Storage Tanks {{usersEight.LO_Isolating_valve03}}
GT-4 Storage Tanks {{usersEight.LO_Isolating_valve04}}
No. 1 DA {{usersEight.LO_Isolating_valve05}}
Aft E/R general supply {{usersEight.LO_Isolating_valve06}}
Fwd E/R general supply {{usersEight.LO_Isolating_valve07}}
(viii) Purifier Feed pump suction and discharge valves Purifier no. 1 {{usersEight.LO_Purifier_valve01}}
Purifier no. 2 {{usersEight.LO_Purifier_valve02}}
(ix) Purifier system inlet, outlet, isolating valves Purifier no. 1 {{usersEight.LO_Purifier_inlet_valve01}}
Purifier no. 2 {{usersEight.LO_Purifier_inlet_valve02}}

(c) Tanks.
Visual/ functional checks to be carried out for the following (for LO storage tank in FER/ AER)

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Sounding tube and sounding tapes – Volumetric marking on sounding chart to be cross checked with sounding tape markings during fuelling. Intactness of sounding tape and tube to be checked during tank cleaning activity. {{usersEight.LO_Tank_valve01}}
(ii) APT - Test certificate to be obtained from respective agencies. {{usersEight.LO_Tank_valve02}}
(iii) Paint scheme. {{usersEight.LO_Tank_valve03}}
(iv) Manhole door gaskets to be renewed. {{usersEight.LO_Tank_valve04}}

(d) Instrumentation.
Visual/ functional checks are to be carried out for the following:

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Pressure gauges.
All pressure gauges in the system and equipment to checked for functionality and operation.
Pressure gauge marking for operating range to be completed.
Calibration certificates to be provided Valves in gauge line and on the pressure, gauge are to be checked for functionality and operation .		 Pump 1 suction pressure gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_01}}
Pump 1 discharge pressure gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_02}}
Pump 2 suction pressure gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_03}}
Pump 2 discharge pressure gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_04}}
Purifier feed pump no. 1 suction gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_05}}
Purifier feed pump no. 1 discharge gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_06}}
Purifier feed pump no. 2 suction gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_07}}
Purifier feed pump no. 2 discharge gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_08}}
Purifier no. 1 outlet pressure gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_09}}
Purifier no. 2 outlet pressure gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_10}}
(ii) Sensor. All sensors in the LO tank to be checked for functionality and operation. Calibration certificates to be provided Storage tank level digital indicator (MCR) {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_20}}
Purifier no.1 running indication {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_11}}
Purifier no.2 running indication {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_12}}
(iv) Gauge lines. All gauge lines to be checked for proper fitting and leakages. DD air compressor discharge pr gauge {{usersEight.LO_instrumentation_pressure_remark_19}}

(e) Foundation.
Visual checks of foundation are to be done for ascertaining foundation condition.

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Foundation GT Lub oil transfer pump 1 {{usersEight.LO_foundation_remark_01}}
GT Lub oil transfer pump 2 {{usersEight.LO_foundation_remark_02}}
RG Lub oil transfer pump 1 {{usersEight.LO_foundation_remark_03}}
RG Lub oil transfer pump 2 {{usersEight.LO_foundation_remark_04}}
Lub oil Purifier 1 {{usersEight.LO_foundation_remark_05}}
Lub oil Purifier 2 {{usersEight.LO_foundation_remark_06}}

(f) Mounting.
Condition, attenuation and grouping of mounts have to be checked.

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Mounting Physical Condition {{usersEight.LO_mounting_remark_01}}
Attenuation {{usersEight.LO_mounting_remark_02}}
Service life as per MAINTOPS/OEM Doc. {{usersEight.LO_mounting_remark_03}}

FIRE MAIN SYSTEM

1. Pre -Requisite Check – Off List for System Trials.

Ser. Description Remarks
(a) Pump installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings. Applicable in case of ABER/ MLU replacement of item. Fire main pump no. 1 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_01}}
Fire main pump no. 2 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_02}}
Fire main pump no. 3 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_03}}
Fire main pump no. 4 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_04}}
Fire main pump no. 5 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_05}}
Fire main pump no. 6 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_06}}
Fire main pump no. 7 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_07}}
Fire main pump no. 8 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_08}}
Fire main pump no. 9 {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_09}}
(b) Pressure gauges are calibrated and red marked working pressure of the system. Pump suction pressure gauges {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_10}}
Pump discharge pressure gauges {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_11}}
Other gauges {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_12}}
(c) System/ installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings. {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_13}}
(d) Sufficient lights available. {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_14}}
(e) Communication available between pump control position and hydrants. (Motorola) {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_15}}
(f) Test area is cleaned. {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_16}}
(g) All the pipes are properly supported with clamps. {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_17}}
(h) Talley plates are fitted on all valves, cocks and gauges. {{usersEight.pre_req_remark_18}}

2. Fire Main Pump.

Carry out following checks before starting the pump.

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Check pipe system is clean and clear from obstruction. {{usersEight.fire_main_pump_remark_01}}
(ii) Rotate the pumps, by hand, in direction indicated on the driving end cover. {{usersEight.fire_main_pump_remark_02}}
(iii) Check that all valves are in the open position. {{usersEight.fire_main_pump_remark_03}}
(iv) Start the pump and check correct rotation. {{usersEight.fire_main_pump_remark_04}}
(v) Check suction and discharge pressure of the pump. {{usersEight.fire_main_pump_remark_05}}

3. System Trials.

(a) Piping.
Visual checks of following components are to be carried out for any leakages or damage.

(i) Compensators {{usersEight.a_piping_remark_01}}
(ii) Flange-connections {{usersEight.a_piping_remark_02}}
(iii) Gaskets {{usersEight.a_piping_remark_03}}

(vii) Supporting clamps - Sufficient supporting clamps to be provided at regular intervals as per NES 865. Clamps should have rubber inserts and adequate fasteners. End connection of supporting frame should have rigid support. {{usersEight.a_piping_remark_15}}

(c) Instrumentation.
Visual/ functional checks are to be carried out for the following:

Ser. Description Remarks
(i) Pressure gauges.
All pressure and vacuum gauges in the system and equipment to checked for functionality and operation.
Pressure gauge marking for operating range to be completed.
Calibration certificates to be provided .		 Pump no.1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 & 9 suction pressure gauge {{usersEight.c_instrumentation_remark_01}}

BILGE PUMPING OUT SYSTEM

1. Pre- Requisite Check – Off List for System Trials.

Ser Description Remarks
(a) Pump installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings. Applicable in case of ABER/ MLU replacement of item. Bilge Pump No. 1 {{usersNine.bilge_out_pump_no_1}}
Bilge Pump No. 2 {{usersNine.bilge_out_pump_no_2}}
Salvage Pump No. 1 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_1}}
Salvage Pump No. 2 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_2}}
Salvage Pump No. 3 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_3}}
Salvage Pump No. 4 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_4}}
Salvage Pump No. 5 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_5}}
Salvage Pump No. 6 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_6}}
Salvage Pump No. 7 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_7}}
Salvage Pump No. 8 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_8}}
Salvage Pump No. 9 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_9}}
Salvage Pump No. 10 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_10}}
Salvage Pump No. 11 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_11}}
Salvage Pump No. 12 {{usersNine.salvage_pump_no_12}}
(b) Pressure and vacuum gauges are calibrated and red marked indicating working pressure of the system {{usersNine.bilge_out_press_vacc}}
(c) System/ installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings {{usersNine.bilge_out_sys_drawings}}
(d) Flexible connections are to be provided at pump suction & discharge flanges. {{usersNine.bilge_out_flex_flang}}
(e) Sufficient lights available near operating locations. {{usersNine.bilge_out_suff_loc}}
(f) All the pipes are properly supported with clamps with rubber inserts. {{usersNine.bilge_out_rubb_inserts}}
(g) Talley plates are fitted on all valves, cocks and gauges. {{usersNine.bilge_out_talley_plates}}

2. Bilge/ Salvage Pumps. Carry out following checks before starting the pump.

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Check pipe system is clean and clear from obstruction. {{usersNine.bilge_sal_pipe_clear_obs}}
(ii) Priming unit is provided for priming of main pump to avoid dry running. {{usersNine.bilge_sal_prim_unit}}
(iii) Rotate the pumps, by hand, in direction indicated on the driving end cover. {{usersNine.bilge_sal_driv_end_cover}}
(iv) Check that the functional position of all valves should correspond with the valve position indicators. {{usersNine.bilge_sal_pos_indic}}
(v) Check that all valves are in the open position. {{usersNine.bilge_sal_open_pos}}
(vi) Start the pump and check correct rotation. {{usersNine.bilge_sal_corr_rot}}

3. System Trials

(a). Piping
Visual checks of following components are to be carried out for any leakages or damage

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Bellows/ flexible connections {{usersNine.syst_trial_bellow_conn}}
(ii) Flange-connections {{usersNine.syst_trial_flange_conn}}
(iii) Gaskets {{usersNine.syst_trial_gasket}}
(iv) Adapters {{usersNine.syst_trial_adapter}}
(v) Suction strainers of bilge pump - for cleanliness and correct fittings. Pump No. 1 {{usersNine.syst_trial_suc_strainer_1}}
Pump No. 2 {{usersNine.syst_trial_suc_strainer_2}}
(vi) System Integrity checks - For any type of leakages. {{usersNine.syst_trial_integrity_check}}
(vii) Supporting clamps - Sufficient supporting clamps to be provided at regular intervals as per NES 865. Clamps should have rubber inserts and adequate fasteners. End connection of supporting frame should have rigid support. {{usersNine.syst_trial_supp_conn}}

BALLASTING/ DE-BALLASTING SYSTEM

Pre -Requisite Check – off List for System Trials.

Ser Description Sat/Unsat

Visual checks of following components are to be carried out for any leakages or damage

Ser Description Sat/Unsat

(ii) Gaskets {{usersNine.syst_trial_ballas_gasket}}
(iii) Adapters {{usersNine.syst_trial_ballas_adapter}}

(v) Supporting clamps - Sufficient supporting clamps to be provided at regular intervals as per NES 865. Clamps should have rubber inserts and adequate fasteners. End connection of supporting frame should have rigid support. {{usersNine.syst_trial_ballas_supp_conn}}

CHILLED WATER SYSTEM

1. Pre- Requisite Check – Off List for System Trials.

Ser Description Remarks
(a) Pump and ATU installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings. Applicable in case of ABER/ MLU replacement of item. Pump No. 1 {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_ATU_pump_1}}
Pump No. 2 {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_ATU_pump_2}}
Pump No. 3 {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_ATU_pump_3}}
Pump No. 4 {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_ATU_pump_4}}
Pump No. 5 {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_ATU_pump_5}}
Pump No. 6 {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_ATU_pump_6}}
HE Inlet Valves {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_ATU_HE_inlet}}
HE Outlet Valves {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_ATU_HE_outlet}}
(b) Pressure gauges and thermometers are calibrated and red marked indicating working pressure of the system. Pump suction pressure gauges {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_press_therm_suc}}
Pump discharge pressure gauges {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_press_therm_disc}}
(c) System/ installation completed in accordance with the reference drawings. Any modifications as part of refit to be incorporated in the drawings. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_press_inst_acc}}
(d) Flexible connections are to be provided at pump suction & discharge flanges. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_disc_flanges}}
(e) Chilled water pipes are not to be laid through tanks which contains oil. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_water_pipe}}
(f) All the pipes are properly supported with clamps with rubber inserts. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_clamp_inst}}
(g) Sufficient lights available near operating locations. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_suff_oper_loc}}
(h) All chilled water pipes are insulated with cold lagging. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_cold_lagg}}
(j) Talley plates are fitted on all valves, cocks and gauges. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_talley_plate}}
(k) Air purging cocks are in shut position and the outlet is led to drain. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_press_purg_drain}}

CHILLED WATER PUMP

(a). Carry out following checks before starting the pump

Ser Description Remarks
(i) Check pipe system is clean and clear from obstruction. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_pump_clear_obs}}
(ii) Prime the pumps by opening the pump suction valve and carry out purging of pump casing (for initial starting). {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_pump_init_start}}
(iii) Rotate the pumps, by hand, in direction indicated on the driving end cover. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_pump_end_cover}}
(iv) Check that the functional position of all valves should correspond with the valve position indicators. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_pump_pos_indic}}
(v) Check that all valves are in the open position. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_pump_open_pos}}
(vi) Start the pump and check correct rotation. {{usersNine.syst_trial_chill_pump_corr_rot}}

3. System Trials

(a).Piping
Visual checks of following components are to be carried out for any leakages or damage

Ser Description Sat/Unsat
(i) Bellows/ flexible connections {{usersNine.syst_trial_pip_bellow_conn}}
(ii) Flange-connections {{usersNine.syst_trial_pip_flange_conn}}
(iii) Gaskets {{usersNine.syst_trial_pip_gasket}}
(iv) Adapters {{usersNine.syst_trial_pip_adapter}}
(v) Suction strainers of bilge pump - for cleanliness and correct fittings. Pump No. 1 {{usersNine.syst_trial_pip_suc_strainer_1}}
Pump No. 2 {{usersNine.syst_trial_pip_suc_strainer_2}}
(vi) System Integrity checks - For any type of leakages. {{usersNine.syst_trial_pip_integrity_check}}
(vii) Supporting clamps - Sufficient supporting clamps to be provided at regular intervals as per NES 865. Clamps should have rubber inserts and adequate fasteners. End connection of supporting frame should have rigid support. {{usersNine.syst_trial_pip_supp_conn}}

(b) Valves
Visual and operational checks of following valves are to be carried out. Operate the valves through their complete range of operations to ensure that the valve position indicator moves with the valve.

Port outlet valve {{usersNine.sys_trial_disc_port_outlet}}

(c) Expansion tank.
Visual/ functional checks are to be carried out for the following. Test certificate to be obtained as applicable.

Ser Description Remarks
(i) Sight glass for level indication to be intact and clear. {{usersNine.sys_trial_exp_tank_sight_glass}}
(ii) Paint scheme. As per Navy Order NO 56/16 {{usersNine.sys_trial_exp_tank_paint_scheme}}

ENGINE ROOM SPRINKLING SYSTEM

1. Pre-Requisite Check – Off List for System Trials.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(a) Pressure gauges are calibrated and red marked indicating working pressure of the system. Sprinkling system gauges in FER {{usersTen.Eng_room_Sprink1}}
Sprinkling system gauges in AER {{usersTen.Eng_room_Sprink2}}

(c) Sufficient lights available. {{usersTen.Eng_room_Sufficient_light}}
(d) Test area is cleaned {{usersTen.Eng_room_test_area_clean}}
(e) All the pipes are properly supported with clamps. {{usersTen.Eng_room_pipes_clamps}}
(f) Talley plates are fitted on all valves, cocks and gauges {{usersTen.Eng_room_talley_plats_gauge}}
(g) All nozzles/ sprinklers to be covered with plastic bags so as to avoid filling of water in the compartment. {{usersTen.Eng_room_nozzle_bag}}

Valves with pneumatic drive Hand operated control valves

Lobby No. 21 (for start and cut off of the system) {{usersTen.Eng_spr_valve02}}
Forward engine room portside Lobby No. 21 (for cut off of the water sprinkling system of gangway) {{usersTen.Eng_spr_valve03}}
(ii) Aft Engine Room Aft Engine Room Starboard Aft engine room, starboard, near the ladder(for start and cut off of the system) {{usersTen.Eng_spr_valve04}}
Aft engine room, portside, near the ladder(for start and cut off of the system) {{usersTen.Eng_spr_valve05}}
Aft engine room, No.3 deck, portside Near GTG port side {{usersTen.Eng_spr_valve06}}
Near GTG starboard side {{usersTen.Eng_spr_valve07}}

2. Sea Water Cooling Pump.

Carry out following checks before starting the pump.

Ser. Description SAT/UNSAT
(i) Check pipe system is clean and clear from obstruction. {{usersTen.Sea_water_cooling_pump1}}
(ii) Prime the pumps by opening the pump suction valve and carry out purging of pump casing (for initial starting {{usersTen.Sea_water_cooling_pump2}}
(iii) Rotate the pumps, by hand, in direction indicated on the driving end cove {{usersTen.Sea_water_cooling_pump3}}
(iv) Check that the functional position of all valves should correspond with the valve position indicators. {{usersTen.Sea_water_cooling_pump4}}
(v) Check that all valves are in the open position. {{usersTen.Sea_water_cooling_pump5}}
(vi) Start the pump and check correct rotation. {{usersTen.Sea_water_cooling_pump6}}

TRIALS STATUS OF HALON FIXED FIRE FIGHTING SYSTEM - INS MYSORE

Ser. Description TRIALS STATUS
Halon (Port Station) Halon (Stbd Station) Halon (Pump & Filter Room)
Control Panel Checks
1 Control Panel lamp test {{usersTen.Halon_system1}}
2 Mains ‘ON’ Indication {{usersTen.Halon_system2}}
3 Mains ‘Fail’ Indication {{usersTen.Halon_system3}}
4 Emergency battery backup checks (CNC Switchboard) {{usersTen.Halon_system4}}

Physical Checks
17 Securing arrangements of all three N2 Pilot bottles and all Nine Halon bottles position in each station. (a) Connection of halon bottles to manifold (Checks of flexible wire braided hose connections between bottle and hose, hose and NRV). (b) Connection of pilot nitrogen bottles to DCVs and Master Halon Cylinder. (c) Securing clamps of Halon Bottles. (d) Securing clamps of system lines {{usersTen.Halon_system39}} {{usersTen.Halon_system40}} {{usersTen.Halon_system41}}
